dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal detects extreme trending days (open at one extreme, close at opposite extreme) and fades the reversal the next day. Entry logic: previous day was strong trending (prev_close vs prev_open directional), current day reverses beyond previous close with volume confirmation. Exit targets the previous day's open (opposite extreme). Stop loss set at 1.5 ATR equivalent using stop_loss_pct. Hold_days_max set to 1 since this is an intraday mean reversion setup. Context-dependent direction: LONG when previous day closes higher (reversal expected downward), SHORT when previous day closes lower (reversal expected upward).

backtest_rule.reason: Signal detects extreme trending days (open at one extreme, close at opposite extreme) and fades the reversal the next day. Entry logic: previous day was strong trending (prev_close vs prev_open directional), current day reverses beyond previous close with volume confirmation. Exit targets the previous day's open (opposite extreme). Stop loss set at 1.5 ATR equivalent using stop_loss_pct. Hold_days_max set to 1 since this is an intraday mean reversion setup. Context-dependent direction: LONG when previous day closes higher (reversal expected downward), SHORT when previous day closes lower (reversal expected upward).

source_rule_text: When the market exhibits a strong trending day (opening at one extreme and closing at the other), expect mean reversion the following day; fade the opening direction momentum in favor of a reversal bias.
